_note: Per-root-cause KEYSTONE: the single highest-impact, lowest-effort daily habit for that root cause (Assessment #5 research). Folded by parse.js onto graph rc.keystone; shown on the protocol page and, later, driven by the Telegram coach. { one: the habit, why: the low-hanging-fruit rationale }. Authored + reviewable; no runtime AI.

patellofemoral-pain.one: One daily isometric hold — a 45-second wall-sit or Spanish squat.
patellofemoral-pain.why: Isometrics quiet kneecap pain almost immediately and need no gym — one hold a day beats a forgotten six-exercise sheet.

patellar-tendinopathy.one: A daily heavy-slow or isometric hold for the tendon.
patellar-tendinopathy.why: Tendons respond to steady daily load; a single timed hold reduces pain and rebuilds capacity without flaring it.

knee-oa.one: A short daily quad-loading session (sit-to-stands or a wall-sit).
knee-oa.why: Motion and quad strength are the best-proven non-drug levers for knee osteoarthritis — and they're free.

weak-posterior-chain.one: Break up sitting — stand and walk 2 minutes every hour.
weak-posterior-chain.why: Prolonged sitting is the aggravator; hourly movement snacks protect the back better than any supplement.

poor-core-stability.one: A daily 5-minute anti-movement core set (dead-bug, bird-dog, plank).
poor-core-stability.why: Control, not crunches — teaching the core to resist movement is what stops the recurring 'tweaks'.

upper-cross.one: A posture reset every 45 minutes — chin tuck + shoulder roll.
upper-cross.why: Desk posture is the driver; a 10-second reset on a timer undoes hours of forward-head load.

rotator-cuff-impinge.one: Daily cuff and scapular control work (band external rotations).
rotator-cuff-impinge.why: A strong, well-timed cuff keeps the shoulder centred so it stops pinching overhead.

glute-weakness.one: 10 glute bridges before you sit down each morning.
glute-weakness.why: 'Sleepy' glutes cause the ache; 30 seconds of activation primes them for the whole day.

lateral-epicondylopathy.one: One daily set of slow eccentric wrist loading.
lateral-epicondylopathy.why: Eccentrics are the proven tendon fix; one slow set a day rebuilds the tendon without over-gripping.

achilles-tendinopathy.one: Daily Alfredson heel-raises, slow and controlled.
achilles-tendinopathy.why: The Alfredson protocol is gold-standard and free; the morning-stiffness trend tells you it's working.

anabolic-resistance.one: Hit protein at every meal (~30 g), aiming ~1.6 g/kg a day.
anabolic-resistance.why: Older muscle needs a bigger protein trigger per meal — spreading it out is the single biggest lever against age-related loss.

poor-glucose-disposal.one: A 10-minute walk after your biggest meal.
poor-glucose-disposal.why: A short post-meal walk blunts the glucose spike ~30% — the best effect-per-minute in all of health, and zero planning.

visceral-adiposity.one: A daily step floor plus protein at each meal.
visceral-adiposity.why: Steps (NEAT) drive more fat loss than most gym plans; protein preserves muscle as the belly fat comes off.

energy-surplus.one: A daily step target plus protein-forward meals.
energy-surplus.why: Steps and protein create a gentle deficit without calorie math — the low-effort path out of surplus.

cortisol-fat.one: A fixed wind-down and a 7-hour sleep target.
cortisol-fat.why: Stress and short sleep drive cortisol belly fat; fixing sleep first does more here than any diet tweak.

iron-anemia.one: Take iron with vitamin C, away from coffee and dairy.
iron-anemia.why: If it's iron, correcting it is transformational — and the only failure mode is bad timing, which this fixes.

mito-inefficiency.one: A daily easy 20-minute walk or zone-2 session.
mito-inefficiency.why: Aerobic base rebuilds mitochondria; keeping it easy means you'll actually do it even when tired.

glycemic-swings.one: A protein-forward breakfast — no naked carbs.
glycemic-swings.why: Stopping the morning glucose spike prevents the afternoon crash-and-craving — fix breakfast, calm the whole day.

subclinical-hypothyroid.one: If on thyroid medication, take it fasted 30–60 min before food, away from minerals and coffee.
subclinical-hypothyroid.why: Thyroid hormone only helps if it's absorbed; timing is the #1 reason people stay symptomatic.

endothelial-sodium.one: Add a potassium-rich food, take a daily walk, and ease off the salt.
endothelial-sodium.why: The potassium-to-sodium ratio plus daily cardio move blood pressure fast, with no prescription.

ldl-fiber.one: 5–10 g of soluble fibre daily (oats or a psyllium scoop).
ldl-fiber.why: Soluble fibre lowers LDL for pennies — one scoop is the cheapest lever there is.

neuroinflammation.one: 10 minutes of morning daylight plus a stable-glucose lunch.
neuroinflammation.why: Light sharpens alertness and steady blood sugar prevents the afternoon fog — both free, both same-day.

gaba-deficit.one: A 2 pm caffeine cut-off, plus a 2-minute paced breath when anxiety spikes.
gaba-deficit.why: Caffeine's long half-life fuels evening anxiety; a paced breath drops arousal in ~90 seconds — the fastest self-rescue there is.

monoamine-inflammation.one: Move your body daily, and log one small win.
monoamine-inflammation.why: Exercise rivals first-line antidepressants; naming a tiny daily win is the evidence-based behavioural lever. Not a substitute for care.

dopamine-deficit.one: A short bout of movement before focused work, plus protein in the morning.
dopamine-deficit.why: A little exercise sharpens attention for ~2 hours and protein supplies dopamine's raw material — stacking the deck before you start.

cholinergic-decline.why: Cardiovascular fitness is the strongest modifiable protector of memory — and walking counts.

hpa-dysregulation.one: One genuine daily downshift, plus morning light.
hpa-dysregulation.why: Burnout recovery needs real downregulation, not more optimisation — the win is making yourself actually stop.

circadian-misalign.one: A fixed wake-time every day, with a wind-down alarm at night.
circadian-misalign.why: Anchoring wake-time self-corrects the whole clock within 1–2 weeks — the single most effective, zero-cost sleep fix.

cortisol-glucose-night.one: Stabilise the evening — no alcohol, and don't eat too late.
cortisol-glucose-night.why: Nighttime cortisol and glucose swings, plus alcohol, drive the 3 am wake; small evening changes fix it.

circadian-desync.one: Time your light and caffeine to your shift — seek light early, avoid it late.
circadian-desync.why: Strategic light timing is the only thing that actually shifts the body clock — the highest-leverage move for shift work.

lifestyle-suppressed-t.one: Sleep 7 hours and lift 3× a week.
lifestyle-suppressed-t.why: Sleep and resistance training are the biggest natural testosterone levers — poor sleep alone can drop T 10–15%.

primary-hypogonadism.one: Stay consistent with treatment and don't miss follow-up bloodwork.
primary-hypogonadism.why: For a confirmed clinical case, adherence and monitoring are where the results and safety live.

estrogen-decline.one: Strength and impact training 2–3× a week, for muscle and bone.
estrogen-decline.why: Muscle and bone are use-it-or-lose-it through menopause — loading protects both better than any pill alone.

vascular-hormonal-libido.one: Prioritise sleep, daily cardio for blood flow, and lowering stress.
vascular-hormonal-libido.why: Libido is downstream of sleep, stress and vascular health — fix the inputs, not the symptom.

low-bmd.one: Load your skeleton — short impact or resistance sessions.
low-bmd.why: Bone only strengthens in response to load; two short weekly sessions beat calcium alone.

insulin-androgen.one: A 10-minute post-meal walk — the insulin-first lever.
insulin-androgen.why: Insulin resistance drives the androgens in PCOS; treating the metabolic root eases the downstream symptoms.

collagen-loss-glycation.one: Sunscreen every morning and a nightly retinoid.
collagen-loss-glycation.why: Sun protection is the #1 anti-ageing intervention, full stop — one tap to confirm; retinoid consistency does the rebuilding.

uv-oxidative.one: Daily sunscreen, reapplied, plus vitamin C in the morning.
uv-oxidative.why: Most 'ageing' skin is UV damage; blocking it is the highest-yield, lowest-effort move for tone and spots.

sebum-insulin.one: A consistent AM/PM routine and a lower-glycemic diet — and don't pick.
sebum-insulin.why: Acne clears on consistency, not intensity — and a food-trigger log finds your personal driver.

dht-sensitivity.one: Minoxidil or finasteride every single day, plus a monthly progress photo.
dht-sensitivity.why: These only work with near-perfect adherence and take months — a daily reminder and a photo timeline is the whole game.

nutrient-deficiency-hair.one: Refeed the follicle — hit protein, iron and zinc; check ferritin.
nutrient-deficiency-hair.why: Diffuse shedding from deficiency reverses once you're properly refed.

cellular-senescence.one: Protect your VO₂max and strength — weekly zone-2 plus two lifts.
cellular-senescence.why: Fitness is the most evidence-backed longevity 'drug'; the win is doing the dose simply and consistently.

mtor-overactivation.one: Keep a consistent daily eating window (time-restricted eating).
mtor-overactivation.why: A steady eating window is the simplest way to cycle growth and repair — one timer, no calorie math.

low-grade-inflammation.one: Omega-3 daily, move your body, and swap one ultra-processed food.
low-grade-inflammation.why: Diet and movement drive 'inflammaging'; one daily swap compounds without a full diet overhaul.

dysbiosis.one: Aim for 30 different plants a week.
dysbiosis.why: Plant diversity is the strongest, most actionable microbiome lever — and counting it is oddly fun and low-effort.

micronutrient-immune.one: Sleep, vitamin D and zinc — kept consistent.
micronutrient-immune.why: The unglamorous foundations do most of the immune work; consistency is the lever.

stimulus-recovery.one: Log one number each session and beat last week's top set.
stimulus-recovery.why: Progressive overload is the only thing that truly breaks a plateau — logging it makes progression visible and honest.

neural-recovery.one: A quick morning readiness check to gate training vs a deload.
neural-recovery.why: Grinding through fatigue stalls strength; a 5-second readiness check stops you digging the hole deeper.

metabolic-adaptation.one: Hold a protein and step floor, and judge by the weekly trend.
metabolic-adaptation.why: Plateaus are usually adherence drift; a weekly trend beats daily noise, and the floors restart the loss.

aerobic-base.one: Add easy zone-2 volume each week (and check your iron).
aerobic-base.why: Most stalls are too little easy volume — plus low iron in endurance athletes; tracking easy minutes fixes the first.

low-readiness.one: Time caffeine ~45 minutes pre-session with a fast carb.
low-readiness.why: Right-timed stimulant and fuel is the cheapest performance jump there is.

autonomic-imbalance.one: A 10-second morning check — resting heart rate and 'did I sleep?' — and take the rest day when it says so.
autonomic-imbalance.why: The fix for overtraining is less; a quick morning check catches the dig and gives you permission to stop.
